content.text:
Let me analyze this record carefully.

The gloss: material = stuff of which a thing consists, such that (a) every part of the thing is of that kind of matter, and (b) someone who makes the thing works on it.

The rendering:
- Ref 1 = the material (a kind of thing)
- Ref 2 = the thing made
- Clause 1: ref1 is a kind of thing
- Clause 2: ALL parts of some thing (ref2) are of the kind (of ref1) — captures "every part is of it"
- Clause 3 (WHEN): when someone makes ref2 (the make-explication inlined as manner), that someone (ref3) does something to some of ref1 (the material) — captures "someone who makes the thing works on the material"

This carries both criterial clauses: universal part-membership + maker acting on the material during making. The self-flag correctly notes losses: continuous-mass substancehood and persistence-through-making exceed the primes; rendered as kind-membership + linkage to making. That is an honest lossy flag.

Gloss is genus-differentia (genus: stuff/matter; differentia: all-parts-of-it + worked-on-in-making), sense-fixed, non-circular.

Explication carries the criterial core. Self-flag is true. No collision.

{"id": "urn:molaug-v0:material",
 "verdict": "ACCEPT",
 "gloss_scholarly": true,
 "explication_carries_gloss": true,
 "self_flag_correct": true,
 "sense_collision_free": true,
 "reason": "Gloss is genus-differentia and non-circular; rendering carries both criterial clauses (all parts of the thing are of the material's kind + the maker acts on the material during making); lossy self-flag honestly names the dropped substancehood/persistence residue."}
